meta.title: Momcozy Storefront Technical Audit
meta.scope: Public storefront homepage and one representative product-detail journey; desktop and mobile
meta.method: Automated Playwright route-aware collection with dual viewport and route aggregation

summary.verdict: The 2026-06-14 automated baseline confirms the previous pattern: the storefront remains technically constrained by large client-side payload, large DOM size, and unstable third-party reliability, while CLS is stable and first paint remains fast. Route-aware capture now proves the same failure modes on homepage and representative product-detail paths, and LCP is still not observable in this lab mode.
strengths: Route-aware automated collection now covers both homepage and product-detail in desktop and mobile in one run
TTFB is within the good envelope on both desktop and mobile for sampled routes
CLS is stable at 0 for both tracked routes and both viewports in the latest run
Data pipeline, confidence scoring, and release checks are complete enough to treat this as a bounded baseline for technical decisions
risks: LCP remains unobserved in all latest route/viewport samples, so actual render-to-interaction sequencing is still missing in lab
Third-party request failures remain high (45-56 per route), which can distort attribution, personalization, and analytics signals
The technical ceiling is still exceeded: ~1.9 MB JS and ~7.3k DOM nodes on sampled routes
Product-detail path introduces extra failure and complexity risk (script tags 342, iframes 32, console/page errors), while image format and accessibility debt remain unresolved
No direct business causality is claimed from this public file; internal operating data is only used to constrain interpretation

metrics.label: LCP observability coverage
metrics.interpretation: No LCP value was observed in 2026-06-14 automated samples (desktop and mobile for homepage and product-detail). The same route-aggregate run reported lcp_unobserved_in_timeout on all samples, so the route is still not a reliable LCP source in headless mode.

metrics.label: First contentful paint (homepage, desktop)
metrics.interpretation: The latest homepage desktop FCP is 0.58 s, which is materially faster than the earlier manual value and indicates the first paint itself is no longer the dominant bottleneck.
metrics.source: Chromium paint timing, session-2026-06-14 homepage desktop

metrics.label: First contentful paint (homepage, mobile)
metrics.interpretation: The homepage mobile FCP is 0.34 s in the latest run, still fast under lab assumptions and well under a common 1.8 s threshold.
metrics.source: Chromium paint timing, session-2026-06-14 homepage mobile

metrics.label: Server response (homepage, desktop)
metrics.interpretation: Homepage desktop TTFB is 416 ms and mobile is 213 ms in the same session, both within the 800 ms practical boundary for the sampled scope.
metrics.source: Navigation timing, session-2026-06-14 homepage desktop + mobile

metrics.label: Server response (product-detail, mobile)
metrics.interpretation: Product-detail mobile TTFB is 298 ms, confirming that server responsiveness is not the primary blocker for deeper-journey speed regressions.
metrics.source: Navigation timing, session-2026-06-14 representative product-detail mobile

metrics.label: Layout shift
metrics.interpretation: CLS remains at 0 across latest sampled routes and both viewports, indicating no major layout instability during initial paint in this instrumentation window.

metrics.label: JavaScript payload (homepage)
metrics.interpretation: Homepage JavaScript transfer is still 1.9 MB on desktop (1.905 MB on mobile), exceeding a practical 500 KB lab target by ~3.8x.
metrics.source: Resource timing API initiatorType=script transferSize sum, session-2026-06-14 homepage

metrics.label: DOM node count (homepage)
metrics.interpretation: The homepage node count remains above 7k (desktop 7356, mobile 8088), a level likely to increase rendering and scripting costs on lower-end devices.

metrics.label: Third-party failures (homepage)
metrics.interpretation: Homepage recorded 45 desktop and 44 mobile third-party failures; this remains materially above the <10 operational budget and can suppress analytics or feature initialization consistency.

metrics.label: Third-party failures (product-detail)
metrics.interpretation: Representative product-detail added pressure with 56 desktop and 55 mobile failures, confirming route-specific fragility and not just homepage-specific script noise.

metrics.label: Product-detail load completion
metrics.interpretation: Product-detail desktop load event reached near 4.94 s, much cleaner than legacy manual snapshots but still materially slower than expected for a conversion-critical route.

metrics.label: Product-detail image format coverage
metrics.interpretation: Product-detail desktop showed 11.08% WebP image coverage in this sample, while homepage remained below 1%; format strategy is route-sensitive and still below a healthy technical floor.
metrics.source: Image scan, session-2026-06-14 product-detail and homepage

metrics.label: Product-detail missing alt text
metrics.interpretation: At least 40 image tags were missing alt on the representative product-detail route sample; this is an accessibility regression candidate in a high-signal path.

forensics.label: Observed third-party request failures
forensics.interpretation: The highest observed route/viewport failure count in 2026-06-14 was 56 on product-detail desktop, with homepage desktop at 45. This is not comparable to the old manual baseline (3) and indicates collector-comparable route failures are now structurally elevated.

forensics.label: Observed long tasks
forensics.interpretation: Main-thread task pressure is measurable but not exploding in this run (2-3). TBT remains low in lab likely due early network failures that stop script execution before blocking phases accumulate.

forensics.label: JavaScript payload — latest finding
forensics.interpretation: Latest product-detail desktop observed 1.942 MB JS. Homepage stays around 1.904 MB. This confirms the JavaScript budget issue is not isolated to one route.
forensics.source: Resource timing API initiatorType=script transferSize sum, session-2026-06-14 routes

forensics.label: DOM node count — latest finding
forensics.interpretation: Homepage mobile reached 8,088 nodes and product-detail climbed to 8,073 on mobile. Route-aware growth remains consistent with earlier manual path observations.

forensics.label: LCP observability risk
forensics.interpretation: All 4 samples (2 routes × 2 viewports) failed to produce LCP. This gives high confidence that hero/background implementation, not bandwidth alone, is the primary cause of LCP invisibility.

forensics.label: Audited journeys
forensics.interpretation: The latest automated scope now covers homepage and one representative product-detail path, still short of full conversion funnel coverage (cart/checkout/search).

forensics.label: Product-detail DOM footprint
forensics.interpretation: The representative product-detail route includes 342 script tags with 32 iframes; this is high surface area for load ordering, hydration, and error propagation.

forensics.label: Product-detail runtime errors
forensics.interpretation: Product-detail captured 4 console errors and 5 page errors in combined desktop/mobile samples. Field-side INP or real-user traces are still required to map business impact, but this is a concrete engineering risk signal.

forensics.label: Image accessibility gaps
forensics.interpretation: Homepage + product-detail combined missing accessibility tags remain a concrete debt: homepage still has alt/alt-like gaps and product-detail has 40 missing alt observations.

recommendations.title: Make hero image LCP-eligible
recommendations.action: Replace CSS-background and lazy-loaded hero mechanisms with an eagerly loaded, sized img candidate (or validated alternative markup) using hero-specific loading priority and preload hints so automated LCP can be observed consistently.
recommendations.validation: Re-run route-aggregate collection and confirm LCP becomes a concrete numeric value in both desktop/mobile and both tracked routes. Repeat across one follow-up run before closing.

recommendations.title: Reduce JavaScript payload below route budget
recommendations.action: Trim app/runtime bundles and postpone non-critical scripts. Aim to keep initial transfer below 600 KB on first paint path; target below 500 KB as a hard warning threshold.
recommendations.validation: Automated re-collect should show jsKb < 600 on homepage desktop and product-detail desktop. Also confirm TBT no longer stays at zero because of blocked script execution alone.

recommendations.title: Control third-party failure surface by route
recommendations.action: Add script guards and defer/load-order constraints for non-essential widgets; keep failures outside critical purchase rendering and checkout initiation paths.
recommendations.validation: Re-run collection and hold 3P failures below 10 for homepage and below 15 for product-detail as an immediate hard gate, with per-route thresholds in CI checks.

recommendations.title: Reduce DOM size on route-level templates
recommendations.action: Audit the highest DOM contributors in product template and homepage modules, with route-specific pruning, virtualization, and lazy mount strategies for off-screen sections.
recommendations.validation: Re-run collection and confirm domNodes < 3000 for homepage and < 5000 for product-detail in at least one representative snapshot.

recommendations.title: Fix product-detail runtime faults and accessibility debt
recommendations.action: Prioritize the 4+ page/console errors and 40 missing-alt gaps on product-detail route, especially around script state initialization and recommendation widgets.
recommendations.validation: Re-run product-detail desktop/mobile and confirm console errors and missing-alt counts drop materially before broad route rollout.

recommendations.title: Extend recurring route coverage
recommendations.action: Add cart and checkout routes to v3 collection scope, with same route filters used in trends and threshold reports.
recommendations.validation: Ensure each new route has normalized desktop/mobile metrics and a methodology tag so trend comparisons stay route-specific.

recommendations.title: Automate image-format and accessibility checks
recommendations.action: Track WebP coverage, missing alt, missing srcset, and above-fold lazy ratios route-by-route in the collector so degradations are caught before deployment.
recommendations.validation: Fail automated quality checks when route-level image-format and accessibility debt regresses beyond review thresholds.

glossary.definition: Time until the largest visible content element is rendered. Must be an img, video poster, or block-level text — CSS backgrounds do not qualify.
Time until the browser renders the first visible content.
A measure of unexpected visual movement during page load. A score of 0 is perfect; good is below 0.1.
Time to First Byte — elapsed time from navigation start to the first byte of the HTTP response. The most consistent metric across lab and real-user conditions.

Total Blocking Time — sum of time each long task blocks the main thread between FCP and Time to Interactive. In lab sessions where third-party scripts fail at the network level, TBT may read low regardless of payload size.
How strongly the limited public evidence supports a finding.
Metrics grouped by sampled route and viewport so homepage and product paths are not mixed into one direct comparison unless explicitly noted.
